summary: Step 4 Unified Header — ChatTopBar + scroll-glow pulse tested on desktop/mobile. All 9 test scenarios PASS after fixing a critical CSS nesting bug in /app/app/globals.css that was breaking the @keyframes scroll-glow-pulse rule in production builds.

test_results.T1_messages_desktop_inbox_ChatTopBar: PASS — app-header-chat visible, height=48px, --app-header-height='48px', has back/logo/title='Messages', no public/workspace headers
test_results.T2_messages_mobile_inbox_ChatTopBar_hidd: PASS — computed display:none via 'hidden lg:flex', not visible at 390x844
test_results.T3_messages_desktop_thread_ChatTopBar: PASS — sticky top:0, z-index:100, height:48px, display:flex
test_results.T4_back_button_navigates_to_inbox: PASS — click on [data-testid=app-header-chat-back] on /messages/test-thread-id → router.push('/messages') → URL=/messages
test_results.T5_scroll_glow_pulse_at_100pct: PASS (after CSS fix + rebuild) — data-complete=true at bottom, animate-scroll-glow-pulse class applied, animationName=scroll-glow-pulse duration=2s, box-shadow animates mid-pulse. data-complete=false after scrolling back to top, class removed.
test_results.T6_prefers_reduced_motion_guard: PASS — under reducedMotion=reduce, animationName=none (static glow box-shadow still applied). Class still present but @media rule overrides animation to none.
test_results.T7_partner_mobile_sidebar_no_legacy_head: PASS — /partner/dashboard redirects to /profile (auth guard expected). Legacy header count=0 (no bg-white+border-b+border-slate-200+z-30+lg:hidden header in DOM). Workspace header rendered in UNIFIED mode.
test_results.T8_admin_mobile_sidebar_no_legacy_dark_h: PASS — /admin redirects to /profile. Legacy dark-gradient header (from-slate-900+lg:hidden) count=0 in DOM. Workspace header rendered.

critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL (FIXED): /app/app/globals.css — the new @keyframes scroll-glow-pulse, .animate-scroll-glow-pulse, and @media (prefers-reduced-motion) rules were inserted INSIDE the .gostaylo-price-pill--selected:hover block (missing closing brace at line 234 before the new block). In the compiled production CSS this manifested as postcss-nesting: '.gostaylo-price-pill--selected:hover { background:...; @keyframes scroll-glow-pulse{...} .animate-scroll-glow-pulse {...} @media ... { ... } }'. As a result: (a) @keyframes rule was scoped to a selector (invalid — keyframes are global) so the animation name was unresolved, (b) the .animate-scroll-glow-pulse utility only applied when an ancestor matched .gostaylo-price-pill--selected:hover (never for the scroll progress bar). Fixed by moving the three rules out of the hover block and closing the hover block properly.

Stale production bundle: frontend supervisor runs 'next start' (production mode). CSS edits to globals.css don't hot-reload — required a full `yarn build` + supervisor restart for the fix to reach the browser. Main agent should account for this when iterating on CSS during the sprint (either switch supervisor to `next dev` for the sprint, or always run `yarn build` after CSS changes).
updated_files: /app/app/globals.css (unbraced hover block + extracted @keyframes / .animate-scroll-glow-pulse / @media prefers-reduced-motion rules to top level)
action_items: Verify the CSS structure in /app/app/globals.css — @keyframes and .animate-scroll-glow-pulse are now top-level (lines 236-249). Frontend was rebuilt (yarn build) and supervisor restarted — browser confirmed animationName='scroll-glow-pulse' at 100% scroll.
Consider adding a lint rule (stylelint) to catch unclosed braces in globals.css before they hit production build.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Step 4 unified header (ChatTopBar at h-12 desktop-only on /messages/*) is working correctly. Scroll-glow-pulse animation is working after fixing a CSS nesting bug. Main blocker discovered & fixed was that globals.css had unclosed .gostaylo-price-pill--selected:hover block causing the @keyframes rule to be nested inside a selector. Also note: frontend runs 'next start' (production), so CSS changes require `yarn build` + `sudo supervisorctl restart frontend`. Partner/admin mobile sidebar legacy headers are correctly absent (count=0 in DOM). UNIFIED_HEADER flag is ON via NEXT_PUBLIC_UNIFIED_HEADER=on.
